    15→FUNCTION
    16→        <<sin>>, <<sinf>>, <<cos>>, <<cosf>>---sine or cosine
    17→INDEX
    18→sin
    19→INDEX
    20→sinf
    21→INDEX
    22→cos
    23→INDEX
    24→cosf
    25→ANSI_SYNOPSIS
    26→        #include <math.h>
    27→        double sin(double <[x]>);
    28→        float  sinf(float <[x]>);
    29→        double cos(double <[x]>);
    30→        float cosf(float <[x]>);
    31→
    32→TRAD_SYNOPSIS
    33→        #include <math.h>
    34→        double sin(<[x]>)
    35→        double <[x]>;
    36→        float  sinf(<[x]>)
    37→        float <[x]>;
    38→
    39→        double cos(<[x]>)
    40→        double <[x]>;
    41→        float cosf(<[x]>)
    42→        float <[x]>;
    43→
    44→DESCRIPTION
    45→	<<sin>> and <<cos>> compute (respectively) the sine and cosine
    46→	of the argument <[x]>.  Angles are specified in radians. 
    47→
    48→	<<sinf>> and <<cosf>> are identical, save that they take and
    49→	return <<float>> values. 
    50→
    51→
    52→RETURNS
    53→	The sine or cosine of <[x]> is returned.
    54→
    55→PORTABILITY
    56→	<<sin>> and <<cos>> are ANSI C. 
    57→	<<sinf>> and <<cosf>> are extensions.
    58→
    59→QUICKREF
    60→	sin ansi pure
    61→	sinf - pure
    62→*/
    63→
    64→/* sin(x)
    65→ * Return sine function of x.
    66→ *
    67→ * kernel function:
    68→ *	__kernel_sin		... sine function on [-pi/4,pi/4]
    69→ *	__kernel_cos		... cose function on [-pi/4,pi/4]
    70→ *	__ieee754_rem_pio2	... argument reduction routine
    71→ *
    72→ * Method.
    73→ *      Let S,C and T denote the sin, cos and tan respectively on 
    74→ *	[-PI/4, +PI/4]. Reduce the argument x to y1+y2 = x-k*pi/2 
    75→ *	in [-pi/4 , +pi/4], and let n = k mod 4.
    76→ *	We have
    77→ *
    78→ *          n        sin(x)      cos(x)        tan(x)
    79→ *     ----------------------------------------------------------
    80→ *	    0	       S	   C		 T
    81→ *	    1	       C	  -S		-1/T
    82→ *	    2	      -S	  -C		 T
    83→ *	    3	      -C	   S		-1/T
    84→ *     ----------------------------------------------------------
    85→ *
    86→ * Special cases:
    87→ *      Let trig be any of sin, cos, or tan.
    88→ *      trig(+-INF)  is NaN, with signals;
    89→ *      trig(NaN)    is that NaN;
    90→ *
    91→ * Accuracy:
    92→ *	TRIG(x) returns trig(x) nearly rounded 
    93→ */
    94→
    95→#pragma GCC optimize ("O2")
    96→#include "fdlibm.h"
    97→
    98→#ifndef _DOUBLE_IS_32BITS
    99→
   100→__int32_t __ieee754_rem_pio2(double, double *);
   101→#ifdef __STDC__
   102→	double sin(double x)
   103→#else
   104→	double sin(x)
   105→	double x;
   106→#endif
   107→{
   108→	double y[2],z=0.0;
   109→	__int32_t n;
   110→
   111→	double ix = __riscv_fabs(x);
   112→
   113→    /* |x| ~< pi/4 */
   114→	if(likely(ix <= M_PI / 4))
   115→            if (unlikely(ix < 0x1p-26 && (int) x == 0))
   116→                return x;
   117→            else
   118→                return __kernel_sin(x,z,0);
   119→
   120→    /* sin(Inf or NaN) is NaN */
   121→	else if (unlikely(__riscv_fclass(ix) & 0x700)) return x-x;
   122→
   123→    /* argument reduction needed */
   124→	else {
   125→	    n = __ieee754_rem_pio2(x,y);
   126→	    switch(n&3) {
   127→		case 0: return  __kernel_sin(y[0],y[1],1);
   128→		case 1: return  __kernel_cos(y[0],y[1]);
   129→		case 2: return -__kernel_sin(y[0],y[1],1);
   130→		default:
   131→			return -__kernel_cos(y[0],y[1]);
   132→	    }
   133→	}
   134→}
   135→
   136→#if defined(HAVE_ALIAS_ATTRIBUTE)
   137→#ifndef __clang__
   138→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wmissing-attributes"
   139→#endif
   140→__strong_reference(sin, _sin);
   141→#endif
   142→
   143→#endif /* _DOUBLE_IS_32BITS */
   144→

     3→ *

     5→ *  SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

    18→ *

    20→ */

    38→    -1.949563623766929906511886482584265500187314705496861877317774185883215997931494e-04f,     //SIN_C3
    39→
    40→    //For cosf polynomial:
    41→    1.0f,  //COS_C1
    42→    -4.999989478137016757327030935768632852012781143541026304540837816323349768666875e-01f,     //COS_C2
    43→    4.165629457842617238353362092016541041535652603456375154392942188742496860024377e-02f,      //COS_C3
    44→    -1.35978231111049428748566568960423202948250988565693107969571193763372093404347e-03f       //COS_C4
    45→};
    46→
    47→    #define CST_LIBM_SIN_C1     (cst_libm_sincosf[0])
    48→    #define CST_LIBM_SIN_C2     (cst_libm_sincosf[1])
    49→    #define CST_LIBM_SIN_C3     (cst_libm_sincosf[2])
    50→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C1     (cst_libm_sincosf[3])
    51→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C2     (cst_libm_sincosf[4])
    52→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C3     (cst_libm_sincosf[5])
    53→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C4     (cst_libm_sincosf[6])
    54→#else
    55→    #define CST_LIBM_SIN_C1     (-1.666665066929417292436220415142244613956015227491999719404711781344783392564922e-01f)
    56→    #define CST_LIBM_SIN_C2     (8.331978663157089651408875887703995477889496917296385733254577121461421466427772e-03f)
    57→    #define CST_LIBM_SIN_C3     (-1.949563623766929906511886482584265500187314705496861877317774185883215997931494e-04f)
    58→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C1     (1.0f)
    59→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C2     (-4.999989478137016757327030935768632852012781143541026304540837816323349768666875e-01f)
    60→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C3     (4.165629457842617238353362092016541041535652603456375154392942188742496860024377e-02f)
    61→    #define CST_LIBM_COS_C4     (-1.35978231111049428748566568960423202948250988565693107969571193763372093404347e-03f)
    62→#endif
    63→
    64→#ifdef __STDC__
    65→	float sinf(float x)
    66→#else
    67→	float sinf(x)
    68→	float x;
    69→#endif
    70→{

    72→    x = ref__mathlib_rredf(x, &q);
    73→
    74→    if (__builtin_expect(q < 0, 0)) { // this signals tiny, inf, or NaN

    77→            return TRIVIAL_RESULT(x);
    78→        else if (k == 0xFF000000)      // inf
    79→            return ERR_INF(x);
    80→        else                           // NaN
    81→            return FLOAT_INFNAN(x);
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    if (DO_SIN) {
    85→        float x2 = x*x;
    86→
    87→        x += x * (x2 * (CST_LIBM_SIN_C1 + x2 * (CST_LIBM_SIN_C2 + x2 * (CST_LIBM_SIN_C3))));

    89→            x = -x;
    90→        return x;
    91→    } else {
    92→        float x2 = x*x;
    93→        x = CST_LIBM_COS_C1 + x2 * (CST_LIBM_COS_C2 + x2 * (CST_LIBM_COS_C3 + x2 * (CST_LIBM_COS_C4)));
    94→        if (NEGATE_COS)
    95→            x = -x;
    96→        return x;
    97→    }
    98→}
    99→#pragma GCC reset_options
   100→
   101→#ifdef _DOUBLE_IS_32BITS
   102→
   103→#ifdef __STDC__
   104→	double sin(double x)
   105→#else
   106→	double sin(x)
   107→	double x;
   108→#endif
   109→{
   110→	return (double) sinf((float) x);
   111→}
   112→
   113→#endif /* defined(_DOUBLE_IS_32BITS) */

input.content:
# Plan: Libm Accuracy Test Suite for Andes-Overridden Functions

## Context

We want to agentically optimize the Andes RISC-V assembly libm functions. To do that safely, we need accuracy tests that cover edge cases for every overridden function. Currently picolibc only has a linkage test (`math-funcs.c`) — no correctness or accuracy verification.

The tests will be **standalone C files** (not using picolibc's meson test framework), so we can compile and run each one individually on QEMU during the optimization loop.

## Andes-Overridden Functions (riscv64, hardfp, double ABI)

From `libm/machine/riscv/meson.build` and `hardfloat/` directory:

### Double-precision (8 functions)
| Function | Source | Override |
|----------|--------|----------|
| sin | hardfloat/s_sin.c | Custom with `__riscv_fabs`, `__riscv_fclass` |
| cos | hardfloat/s_cos.c | Same pattern as sin |
| sincos | hardfloat/s_sincos.c | Combined sin+cos |
| atan | hardfloat/s_atan.c | |
| fabs | hardfloat/s_fabs.c | Uses `__riscv_fabs` intrinsic |
| k_sin | hardfloat/k_sin.c | Kernel helper (internal) |
| k_cos | hardfloat/k_cos.c | Kernel helper (internal) |
| e_rem_pio2 | hardfloat/e_rem_pio2.c | Range reduction (internal) |

### Single-precision (8 functions)
| Function | Source |
|----------|--------|
| sinf | hardfloat/sf_sin.c | ARM Optimized Routines based, polynomial approx |
| cosf | hardfloat/sf_cos.c |
| sincosf | hardfloat/sf_sincos.c (C file, separate build) |
| atanf | hardfloat/sf_atan.c |
| logf | hardfloat/sf_log.c |
| expf | hardfloat/sf_exp.c |
| powf | hardfloat/sf_pow.c |
| fabsf | hardfloat/sf_fabs.c |

### Common C overrides (both precisions, ~20 functions)
| Function | Type |
|----------|------|
| copysign/copysignf | Bit manipulation |
| finite/finitef | Classification |
| isinf/isinff | Classification |
| isnan/isnanf | Classification |
| fpclassify (double/float) | Classification |
| fma/fmaf | Fused multiply-add |
| fmax/fmin, fmaxf/fminf | Min/max |
| sqrt/sqrtf | Square root |
| lrint/lrintf, llrint/llrintf | Rounding to int |
| lround/lroundf, llround/llroundf | Rounding to int |

## Test Design

### Structure
```
test/libm-accuracy/
  test-sin.c          # sin + sinf
  test-cos.c          # cos + cosf
  test-sincos.c       # sincos + sincosf
  test-atan.c         # atan + atanf
  test-exp.c          # expf (float only for riscv64)
  test-log.c          # logf (float only for riscv64)
  test-pow.c          # powf (float only for riscv64)
  test-fabs.c         # fabs + fabsf
  test-sqrt.c         # sqrt + sqrtf
  test-fma.c          # fma + fmaf
  test-fmax-fmin.c    # fmax/fmin + fmaxf/fminf
  test-copysign.c     # copysign + copysignf
  test-classify.c     # finite, isinf, isnan, fpclassify (both)
  test-round.c        # lrint/llrint/lround/llround (both)
  test-common.h       # Shared macros: CHECK_DOUBLE, CHECK_FLOAT, CHECK_INT, etc.
  run-all.sh          # Compile and run all tests via QEMU
  Makefile            # Build rules using picolibc specs
```

### Reference Values: Precomputed Golden Values
- Generate on host using Python + mpmath (arbitrary precision)
- Script: `test/libm-accuracy/gen-golden.py` — generates C header with test vectors
- Each function gets a struct array: `{ input, expected_output, max_ulp }`

### Edge Cases Per Category

**Trig (sin, cos, sincos, atan):**
- Zero: ±0.0
- Tiny: subnormal, 1e-300, -1e-300
- Special angles: π/6, π/4, π/3, π/2, π, 3π/2, 2π
- Large args: 1e10, 1e20, 1e100 (tests range reduction)
- Near multiples of π/2 (cancellation-sensitive)
- ±Inf → NaN
- NaN → NaN

**Exp/Log (expf, logf, powf):**
- Zero, ±Inf, NaN
- exp(0)=1, exp(1)=e, exp(-inf)=0, exp(+inf)=+inf
- log(1)=0, log(e)=1, log(0)=-inf, log(-1)=NaN
- pow(0,0), pow(1,inf), pow(2,10)=1024
- Near overflow/underflow boundaries

**Basic (fabs, sqrt, copysign, fmax/fmin):**
- ±0.0, ±Inf, NaN
- sqrt(0), sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(inf), sqrt(-1)=NaN
- copysign with NaN, ±0
- fmax/fmin with NaN (IEEE 754: return the non-NaN)

**Classification (finite, isinf, isnan, fpclassify):**
- Normal, subnormal, zero, inf, NaN for each

**Rounding (lrint, llrint, lround, llround):**
- Exact integers, halfway cases (x.5), near INT_MAX/INT_MIN
- ±0.0, ±Inf, NaN
- Rounding mode sensitivity (for lrint)

### test-common.h Macros
```c
#define CHECK_DOUBLE(func, input, expected, max_ulp)
#define CHECK_FLOAT(func, input, expected, max_ulp)
#define CHECK_DOUBLE_NAN(func, input)
#define CHECK_DOUBLE_INF(func, input, sign)
#define CHECK_INT(func, input, expected)
```

Each macro:
- Calls the function
- Compares result against expected (bit-exact or within ULP tolerance)
- Prints PASS/FAIL with function name, input, expected, got, ULP error
- Increments pass/fail counters

### ULP Calculation
```c
// Compare two doubles, return ULP distance
uint64_t ulp_distance_double(double a, double b);
uint32_t ulp_distance_float(float a, float b);
```

### Tolerances
- **fabs, copysign, fmax/fmin, classify, rounding**: Exact (0 ULP)
- **sqrt, fma**: 0.5 ULP (correctly rounded per IEEE 754)
- **sin, cos, atan, exp, log, pow**: ≤ 1 ULP (faithfully rounded)
- **sincos**: same as sin/cos individually

## Build & Run

### Standalone (outside meson)
```sh
# Compile one test
riscv64-elf-gcc --specs=picolibc.specs -o test-sin test-sin.c -lm
# Run via QEMU
qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic -kernel test-sin
```

### Integration with build_mculib3.sh
Add `--test-accuracy` option that:
1. Builds picolibc normally
2. Compiles each test-*.c against the built picolibc
3. Runs each on QEMU
4. Reports summary

## Implementation Steps

1. Create `test/libm-accuracy/test-common.h` — ULP comparison, CHECK macros, main framework
2. Create `test/libm-accuracy/gen-golden.py` — generates golden values using mpmath
3. Create test files one by one, starting with `test-sin.c` as the template
4. Create `Makefile` and `run-all.sh`
5. Verify all tests pass against current (unoptimized) build
6. Add `--test-accuracy` to `build_mculib3.sh`

## Verification
1. Build picolibc with `--test`  (existing meson tests still pass)
2. Run `run-all.sh` — all accuracy tests pass
3. Intentionally break one function (e.g., return 0 from sin) — verify test catches it


### Reference Values: Precomputed Golden Values
- Generate on host using Python + mpmath (arbitrary precision)
- Script: `test/libm-accuracy/gen-golden.py` — generates C header with test vectors
- Each function gets a struct array: `{ input, expected_output, max_ulp }`

### Edge Cases Per Category

**Trig (sin, cos, sincos, atan):**
- Zero: ±0.0
- Tiny: subnormal, 1e-300, -1e-300
- Special angles: π/6, π/4, π/3, π/2, π, 3π/2, 2π
- Large args: 1e10, 1e20, 1e100 (tests range reduction)
- Near multiples of π/2 (cancellation-sensitive)
- ±Inf → NaN
- NaN → NaN

**Exp/Log (expf, logf, powf):**
- Zero, ±Inf, NaN
- exp(0)=1, exp(1)=e, exp(-inf)=0, exp(+inf)=+inf
- log(1)=0, log(e)=1, log(0)=-inf, log(-1)=NaN
- pow(0,0), pow(1,inf), pow(2,10)=1024
- Near overflow/underflow boundaries

**Basic (fabs, sqrt, copysign, fmax/fmin):**
- ±0.0, ±Inf, NaN
- sqrt(0), sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(inf), sqrt(-1)=NaN
- copysign with NaN, ±0
- fmax/fmin with NaN (IEEE 754: return the non-NaN)

**Classification (finite, isinf, isnan, fpclassify):**
- Normal, subnormal, zero, inf, NaN for each

**Rounding (lrint, llrint, lround, llround):**
- Exact integers, halfway cases (x.5), near INT_MAX/INT_MIN
- ±0.0, ±Inf, NaN
- Rounding mode sensitivity (for lrint)

### test-common.h Macros
```c
#define CHECK_DOUBLE(func, input, expected, max_ulp)
#define CHECK_FLOAT(func, input, expected, max_ulp)
#define CHECK_DOUBLE_NAN(func, input)
#define CHECK_DOUBLE_INF(func, input, sign)
#define CHECK_INT(func, input, expected)
```

Each macro:
- Calls the function
- Compares result against expected (bit-exact or within ULP tolerance)
- Prints PASS/FAIL with function name, input, expected, got, ULP error
- Increments pass/fail counters

### ULP Calculation
```c
// Compare two doubles, return ULP distance
uint64_t ulp_distance_double(double a, double b);
uint32_t ulp_distance_float(float a, float b);
```

### Tolerances
- **fabs, copysign, fmax/fmin, classify, rounding**: Exact (0 ULP)
- **sqrt, fma**: 0.5 ULP (correctly rounded per IEEE 754)
- **sin, cos, atan, exp, log, pow**: ≤ 1 ULP (faithfully rounded)
- **sincos**: same as sin/cos individually

## Build & Run

### Standalone (outside meson)
```sh
# Compile one test
riscv64-elf-gcc --specs=picolibc.specs -o test-sin test-sin.c -lm
# Run via QEMU
qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic -kernel test-sin
```

### Integration with build_mculib3.sh
Add `--test-accuracy` option that:
1. Builds picolibc normally
2. Compiles each test-*.c against the built picolibc
3. Runs each on QEMU
4. Reports summary

## Implementation Steps

1. Create `test/libm-accuracy/test-common.h` — ULP comparison, CHECK macros, main framework
2. Create `test/libm-accuracy/gen-golden.py` — generates golden values using mpmath
3. Create test files one by one, starting with `test-sin.c` as the template
4. Create `Makefile` and `run-all.sh`
5. Verify all tests pass against current (unoptimized) build
6. Add `--test-accuracy` to `build_mculib3.sh`

## Verification
1. Build picolibc with `--test`  (existing meson tests still pass)
2. Run `run-all.sh` — all accuracy tests pass
3. Intentionally break one function (e.g., return 0 from sin) — verify test catches it

# Plan: Libm Accuracy Test Suite for Andes-Overridden Functions

## Context

We want to agentically optimize the Andes RISC-V assembly libm functions. To do that safely, we need accuracy tests that cover edge cases for every overridden function. Currently picolibc only has a linkage test (`math-funcs.c`) — no correctness or accuracy verification.

The tests will be **standalone C files** (not using picolibc's meson test framework), so we can compile and run each one individually on QEMU during the optimization loop.

## Andes-Overridden Functions (riscv64, hardfp, double ABI)

From `libm/machine/riscv/meson.build` and `hardfloat/` directory:

### Double-precision (8 functions)
| Function | Source | Override |
|----------|--------|----------|
| sin | hardfloat/s_sin.c | Custom with `__riscv_fabs`, `__riscv_fclass` |
| cos | hardfloat/s_cos.c | Same pattern as sin |
| sincos | hardfloat/s_sincos.c | Combined sin+cos |
| atan | hardfloat/s_atan.c | |
| fabs | hardfloat/s_fabs.c | Uses `__riscv_fabs` intrinsic |
| k_sin | hardfloat/k_sin.c | Kernel helper (internal) |
| k_cos | hardfloat/k_cos.c | Kernel helper (internal) |
| e_rem_pio2 | hardfloat/e_rem_pio2.c | Range reduction (internal) |

### Single-precision (8 functions)
| Function | Source |
|----------|--------|
| sinf | hardfloat/sf_sin.c | ARM Optimized Routines based, polynomial approx |
| cosf | hardfloat/sf_cos.c |
| sincosf | hardfloat/sf_sincos.c (C file, separate build) |
| atanf | hardfloat/sf_atan.c |
| logf | hardfloat/sf_log.c |
| expf | hardfloat/sf_exp.c |
| powf | hardfloat/sf_pow.c |
| fabsf | hardfloat/sf_fabs.c |

### Common C overrides (both precisions, ~20 functions)
| Function | Type |
|----------|------|
| copysign/copysignf | Bit manipulation |
| finite/finitef | Classification |
| isinf/isinff | Classification |
| isnan/isnanf | Classification |
| fpclassify (double/float) | Classification |
| fma/fmaf | Fused multiply-add |
| fmax/fmin, fmaxf/fminf | Min/max |
| sqrt/sqrtf | Square root |
| lrint/lrintf, llrint/llrintf | Rounding to int |
| lround/lroundf, llround/llroundf | Rounding to int |

## Test Design

### Structure
```
test/libm-accuracy/
  test-sin.c          # sin + sinf
  test-cos.c          # cos + cosf
  test-sincos.c       # sincos + sincosf
  test-atan.c         # atan + atanf
  test-exp.c          # expf (float only for riscv64)
  test-log.c          # logf (float only for riscv64)

  test-sqrt.c         # sqrt + sqrtf
  test-fma.c          # fma + fmaf
  test-fmax-fmin.c    # fmax/fmin + fmaxf/fminf
  test-copysign.c     # copysign + copysignf
  test-classify.c     # finite, isinf, isnan, fpclassify (both)
  test-round.c        # lrint/llrint/lround/llround (both)
  test-common.h       # Shared macros: CHECK_DOUBLE, CHECK_FLOAT, CHECK_INT, etc.

  Makefile            # Build rules using picolibc specs
```
